LAW AND MOTION, 301, PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO'S MOTION TO FILE CONSOLIDATED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILE A MOTION TO FILE A CONSOLIDATED COMPLAINT IN THE CASES LISTED ABOVE. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Consolidated Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-04-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO'S Motion To File Consolidated Complaint

Deny without prejudice to refile a motion to file a consolidated complaint in the cases listed above. If Plaintiff elects to refile the motion, she must submit a memorandum of points and authorities supporting the consolidation, a proposed consolidated complaint, and show proof of serv ice on all affected parties and attorneys.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CM)
